title: Sidecar Server Initialization Path — Complete Map
methodology: Traced via smart_grep, read_source on 20+ files, dependency graph analysis via compass subagent, direct inspection of desktop sidecar process

1_sidecar_entrypoint.process_model: Electron main process spawns sidecar.ts as a separate child process (Bun/Node). Communication is via process.parentPort IPC messages. The sidecar waits for a start command before initializing anything.

lines.parent_port_setup: lines 44-47: type ParentPort, getParentPort() reads process.parentPort
lines.start_function: lines 71-181: async function start(command) — the main init orchestrator with phased error handling
lines.fail_function: lines 74-116: const fail = (phase, error) — writes sidecar-boot-failure.json + IPC message, then process.exit(1)
lines.stop_function: lines 183-188: async function stop() — calls listener?.stop(), posts 'stopped', process.exit(0)

init_phases.what: useSystemCertificates — merges system CA certs into Node TLS defaults
Dynamic import('virtual:opencode-server') — triggers Effect runtime, DB init, config parse, native module loading
Server.listen({ port, hostname, username, password, cors }) — starts HTTP server

server_module_resolution.source: packages/opencode/src/node.ts — barrel re-export of { Config, Server, bootstrap, Log, Database, applyMigrations }

2_config_load.layer_definition: line 381: export const layer = Layer.effect(Service, Effect.gen(function* () { ... }))
config_files_checked: Global config: ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json (or OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR override) — line 339-347: globalConfigFile()
Directory-tree config: scans upward from working directory for openencode.json / openencode.jsonc — line 391-445: readConfigFile()
2_config_load.config_merge_strategy: line 51-53: mergeConfig uses remeda's mergeDeep for deep merging arrays concat via mergeConfigConcatArrays

config_parse_failure.errors: ConfigDirectoryTypoError (line 375-378), ConfigParseError (custom NamedError), $schema injection on write
config_parse_failure.handling: readConfigFile catches parse errors and logs warnings. Invalid files are skipped. The config layer construction yields errors in Effect; callers must handle via Effect.catchTag.
config_parse_failure.schema_injection: line 448: auto-injects $schema: 'https://opencode.ai/config.json' into JSONC files on save

2_config_load.load_order: Config loads AFTER Auth (reads auth.json), Account, Env, AppFileSystem, Npm. No circular dependency.

db_pg_ts.role: Database initialization module — imported as #db (per package.json imports)
db_pg_ts.init_function: line 33-64: export function init(connectionStringOrOpts, ssl?) — creates PGlite or Pool
db_pg_ts.pglite_path: line 39: :memory: or filesystem path (stripped of file: prefix)
db_pg_ts.postgres_path: line 51-63: creates node-postgres Pool with configured poolSize, ssl, timeouts
db_pg_ts.error_handling: line 11-17: DatabaseInitError class with cause + dataDir details. Catches PGlite init failures and wraps.
db_pg_ts.migrations: line 70-108: applyMigrations(db) — idempotent via __drizzle_migrations tracking table for PGlite, built-in migrator for node-postgres

db_ts.role: Non-Effect singleton wrapper — lazy-creates drizzle client via Client() factory.
db_ts.client_factory: line 64-81: export const Client — lazy singleton, calls init(dbPath) on first access
db_ts.db_path_resolution: line 42-52: getPath() — checks Flag.OPENCODE_DB, then channel path, then Global.Path.data/opencode.db
db_ts.transaction_context: line 99-111: use() / transaction() propagate tx via LocalContext (AsyncLocalStorage v2)

adapter_ts.role: Effect Service — @opencode/DatabaseAdapter — wraps the legacy singleton
adapter_ts.defaultLayer: line 386-397: Layer.unwrap(Layer.effect(...)) — reads DatabaseConfig, selects PgAdapter or LocalPgAdapter
adapter_ts.local_adapter: line 86-163: makeLocalPgAdapter — wraps Database.use() (PGlite singleton) via Effect.tryPromise
adapter_ts.pg_adapter: line 185-305: makePgAdapter — creates node-postgres client, with retry (Schedule.forever), health checks, tx isolation
adapter_ts.duckdb_adapter: line 309-385: makeDuckDBAdapter — read-only DuckDB adapter (DYNAMICALLY loaded via import(), not in default path)
adapter_ts.health_integration: Registers database health with HealthRegistry via ping query

3_storage_db_init.init_flow: First call to Database.Client() → getPath() → resolve path → init(dbPath) from db.pg.ts → new PGlite(dataDir) → drizzle({ client })

init_failure.pglite_failure: db.pg.ts:46-49: throws DatabaseInitError with cause and dataDir info
init_failure.migration_failure: applyMigrations does not catch errors — bubbles up to caller. runMigrations in migrate.ts catches at CLI level (process.exit(1))
init_failure.adapter_health: adapter.ts: checkPgHealth() returns { status: 'unhealthy', message } on failure, registered with HealthRegistry

runtime_flags.disable_channel_db: Disables channel-specific DB path (uses plain opencode.db)
runtime_flags.skip_migrations: Skips migration step (flag read in db.ts:27-32)

4_duckdb_init.init_timing: LAZILY initialized — NOT at startup in default path. Only when DuckDBAdapter is explicitly requested.
4_duckdb_init.client_creation: Spawns `duckdb` CLI binary subprocess with -json flag. Falls back to WASM (@duckdb/duckdb-wasm). Read-only by default.
4_duckdb_init.write_layer: Separate DuckDBWrite service layer for DDL (no -readonly flag); used by pipeline.ts and schema.duckdb.ts
4_duckdb_init.pipeline: packages/opencode/src/storage/pipeline.ts — runPipeline(): 1. create tables, 2. bulk insert from adapter events, 3. create analytical views

5_plugin_initialization.layer_definition: line 197: export const layer = Layer.effect(Service, Effect.gen(function* () { ... }))
plugin_loading: line 123-139: internalPlugins() — hardcoded internal plugins: Codex, Copilot, GitLab, Poe, Cloudflare, Azure, DigitalOcean, xAI
line 123-139: internalPlugins reads RuntimeFlags for feature flags
line 167-181: applyPlugin() — dynamic import + hook registration + auth setup
line 152-165: getLegacyPlugins() — scans module exports for legacy plugin patterns
5_plugin_initialization.timing: Plugin layer is merged into AppLayer (app-runtime.ts:72) BEFORE Config loads fully (Config.defaultLayer is provided independently). In sidecar boot: Plugin is initialized when the AppLayer is first used, which happens during server import (phase 5) when Server.listen creates the listener layer.
5_plugin_initialization.failure_handling: publishPluginError (line 212) posts error to bus. Plugin crashes recorded in plugin-health store (loadPluginHealthStore / savePluginHealthStore). Backoff: 30s after first crash, 5min after second, 30min after, then 1hr max (quarantine).

6_mcp_initialization.layer_definition: MCP Service layer (export const layer = Layer.effect(...), line ~1040)
6_mcp_initialization.server_connection_timing: MCP server connections are created LAZILY — when tools() / prompts() / resources() is first called, NOT at layer construction time.
6_mcp_initialization.oauth_flow: OAuth auth happens on-demand via startAuth/authenticate/finishAuth. Supports StreamableHTTP with OAuth client credentials.
6_mcp_initialization.connection_guard: Per-server semaphore (serverLocks Map) prevents concurrent connect/disconnect cycles.
6_mcp_initialization.timing_relative_to_plugins: MCP layer is composed AFTER Plugin in AppLayer (line 74 for Plugin.defaultLayer, line 99 for MCP.defaultLayer), but both are lazy. Plugin hooks fire first during initialization, then MCP connections are established on first use.

native_dependencies.note: Bun-native PTY (only used when Bun runtime)

7_native_module_loading.arch_detection: packages/opencode/bin/opencode:76-147 — supportsAvx2() checks CPU features, findBinary() walks node_modules for platform-specific binary

8_http_server_start.listen_function: line 75-80: export async function listen(opts: ListenOptions): Promise<Listener>

port_resolution.port_0_fallback: line 125: prefers 4096 first, then 0 (any free port) via Effect.catch

8_http_server_start.layer_composition: line 103-118: listenerLayer(opts, port) — HttpRouter.serve + WebSocketTracker.layer + serverLayer + ConfigProvider.layer (for fresh env reads)
8_http_server_start.server_creation: line 192-218: function serverLayer(opts) — createServer() from node:http, wrapped in NodeHttpServer.layer with graceful shutdown (1 second)
8_http_server_start.mdns: line 158-170: setupMdns — Bonjour/mDNS service discovery publishing (optional, only when not loopback)
8_http_server_start.web_handler: packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/server.ts:251-258: webHandler = lazy(() => HttpRouter.toWebHandler(routes, { memoMap, middleware: disposeMiddleware }))
8_http_server_start.api_context: server.ts:99: export const context = Context.makeUnsafe<unknown>(new Map()) — shared context for all HTTP handlers
8_http_server_start.route_services_provided: server.ts:197-245: createRoutes() provides ~50+ service layers (Account, Agent, Auth, Config, LSP, MCP, Plugin, Session, Workspace, etc.)

sidecar_level.phased_error_handler: lines 74-116: const fail = (phase, error) — writes sidecar-boot-failure.json to userDataPath + IPC error message + process.exit(1)
sidecar_level.error_types: Generic Error wrapped as-is (error instanceof Error ? error : new Error(String(error)))
sidecar_level.durable_diagnostic: line 79-100: writeFileSync to <userDataPath>/sidecar-boot-failure.json BEFORE IPC attempt — survives broken IPC
sidecar_level.stderr_capture: lines 60-68: Last 20 lines of process.stderr captured for diagnostic tail

startup_trace.classifier: line 151-191: classifyError(error, phase) — regex-based classification of error messages into typed failure codes
startup_trace.secrets_redaction: line 203-213: redactSecrets() — strips authorization, api_key, token, secret, password, Bearer, Basic, private keys

db_level.db_pg_ts: line 11-17: DatabaseInitError typed error class wrapping PGlite init failures
db_level.adapter_ts: line 11-25: DatabaseError, RetriableDatabaseError, FatalDatabaseError — typed tagged errors
db_level.config_level: Config layer yields Effect errors; DatabaseConfig uses Layer.orDie (panic on missing env)
db_level.migration_level: applyMigrations does NOT catch errors — bubbles up. CLI-level migration script exits with code 1.

10_log_file_paths.init_function: line 66-80: export async function init(options: Options) — sets logpath, truncates if not dev mode, creates write stream

log_file_pattern.truncation: Dev mode: only truncates on first init of a new OPENCODE_RUN_ID. Prod mode: always truncates.
log_file_pattern.retention: Keeps last 10 log files. Older ones are deleted on init via cleanup() (line 89-101).

10_log_file_paths.logger_level: sidecar.ts:159: Log.init({ level: 'WARN' }) — WARN level in production
10_log_file_paths.write_target: File only (options.print=false from sidecar). Write stream via createWriteStream with 'a' flag.
10_log_file_paths.sidecar_stderr: sidecar.ts:60-68: Last 20 lines of stderr captured in memory for diagnostic tail, written to sidecar-boot-failure.json on crash

layers: Config → DatabaseAdapter → Plugin → ShareNext → Format → LSP → File → FileWatcher → Vcs → Snapshot → Bus → Observability
BootstrapRuntime.purpose: Lightweight runtime for early bootstrap before full app is ready

AppRuntime.layers: 55 service layers merged + DatabaseConfig + InstanceLayer (dynamic import of InstanceBootstrap) + Observability + GitHubService
AppRuntime.purpose: Full application runtime for HTTP server, CLI, and ACP agents

init_sequence_diagram: 1. Desktop (Electron main) spawns sidecar process (Bun/Node) → sidecar.ts runs
2. sidecar.ts: parentPort.on('message') → parseCommand → start(command)
3. Phase 1 (line 118): prepareSidecarEnv — set OPENCODE_SERVER_USERNAME/PASSWORD, XDG_STATE_HOME
4. Phase 2 (line 126): ensureLoopbackNoProxy — NO_PROXY loopback entries
5. Phase 3 (line 133): useSystemCertificates — merge system CA certs
6. Phase 4 (line 140): useEnvProxy — http.setGlobalProxyFromEnv()
7. Phase 5 (line 144): dynamic import('virtual:opencode-server') → resolves to opencode/dist/node/node.js
7a. Node module load triggers: import './init-projectors' → SyncEvent.init() with session projectors
7b. AppRuntime.ManagedRuntime.make(AppLayer) — builds entire Effect dependency tree
7c. AppLayer construction creates all 55+ service layers (Config, DB, Plugin, MCP, etc.)
7d. Config.defaultLayer loads opencode.json from directory tree + global config
7e. DatabaseAdapter.defaultLayer: checks OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL → PGlite or PG pool
7f. Plugin.defaultLayer registers internal plugins, loads user plugins
7g. MCP.defaultLayer sets up MCP infrastructure (connections are lazy)
8. Phase 6 (line 159): Log.init({ level: 'WARN' }) → create log file at XDG_DATA/opencode/log/
9. Phase 7 (line 164): Server.listen({ port, hostname, username, password, cors })
9a. HttpApiApp.createRoutes(opts) → builds route tree with ~50 service layers
9b. listenerLayer(opts, port) → HttpRouter.serve + WebSocketTracker + serverLayer
9c. serverLayer creates http.createServer(), wraps in NodeHttpServer.layer
9d. ConfigProvider.layer with fromEnv() — fresh env snapshot per listener
9e. startWithPortFallback → startListener → Layer.buildWithMemoMap → effect runs
9f. NodeHttpServer starts listening on port → TCP address resolved
9g. Optional: mDNS/Bonjour service published
10. parentPort.postMessage({ type: 'ready' }) → desktop knows sidecar is alive
